description_long: This is a single-story residential house with a brick lower facade and white upper walls, featuring a complex, multi-sectioned layout. The house has two prominent covered porches with gable roofs supported by columns, one at the front and one at the side, providing sheltered entryways. Large bay windows are visible on the front corners, allowing for ample natural light and adding architectural interest. The roof is primarily gabled, with multiple intersecting sections and overhangs, and the house includes several rectangular and bay windows arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern. The main entryway is accentuated by steps leading up to a covered porch, flanked by brick columns and a recessed doorway. The overall design suggests a blend of Craftsman and Colonial Revival influences, with a focus on symmetry, brickwork, and prominent porches.
description: A single-story brick and white house with gabled roofs, large bay windows, and two covered porches supported by columns. The design features symmetrical window placement and a prominent main entryway with steps and a sheltered porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch with steps, two large bay windows, and the prominent gabled roof over the porch.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended covered porch, brickwork, and a mix of rectangular and bay windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two covered porches, symmetrical window arrangement, and the main living area with large windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the long brick wall, additional windows, and the side porch with its own gabled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a covered porch with steps, brick columns, and a recessed door under a gabled roof.
